c00.name: A Call Beyond
c00.text: Deal 2 {dmg} to all Enemies in your space.
c00.flavor: Creates a small exploding star, a powerful part of the Choir's arsenal.

c01.name: Augur of Ebrietas
c01.text: Deal 2 {dmg} to 1 Enemy in your space and move them up to 2 spaces away.
c01.flavor: Uses phantasms to partially summon abandoned Ebrietas.

c02.name: Caryll Rune: Beast
c02.text: Deal 2 {dmg} to 1 other Enemy within 1 space.
c02.flavor: "Beast" is one of the early Caryll Runes, as well as one of the first to be deemed forbidden.

c03.name: Beast Roar
c03.text: Move all Enemies in your space up to 2 spaces away.
c03.flavor: Borrow the strength of the terrible undead darkbeasts to blast surrounding foes back.

c04.name: Blood of Adella
c04.text:
Heal 1
May spend 1 Blood Echo to Heal +2.
c04.flavor: Blood taken from Adella, nun of the Healing Church.

c05.name: Blood of Arianna
c05.flavor: Blood taken from Arianna, Cathedral Ward woman of pleasure.

c06.name: Caryll Rune: Blood Rapture
c06.flavor: Transcribes inhuman sounds. "Blood Rapture" is the raw euphoria of the warmth of blood.

c07.name: Blood Stone Shard
c07.text: Place on an Attack Slot. May switch slot on Transform. While on slot, that Attack deals +1 {dmg}.
c07.flavor: At the workshop, these bloodstones are embedded in weapons to fortify them.

c08.name: Caryll Rune: Clawmark
c08.text: Replace your Stat card's effect with: +1 {dmg}, Stagger.
c08.flavor: The "Clawmark" is an impulse to seek the warmth of blood like a beast.

c09.name: Caryll Rune: Communion
c09.flavor: This rune represents the Healing Church and its ministers. Blood ministration is, of course, the pursuit of communion.

c10.name: Empty Phantasm Shell
c10.flavor: Shells with slime harbouring arcane power, which can be rubbed on weapons to imbue them with their strength.

c11.name: Executioner's Gloves
c11.text: Slay 1 Enemy within 2 spaces that has 2 or less remaining HP.
c11.flavor: The gloves of an executioner from a faraway land.

c12.name: Caryll Rune: Eye
c12.text: Discard 1 card from your hand to return 1 card from your discard.
c12.flavor: A transcription of "Eye," as spoken by left-behind Great Ones. Allows one to make additional discoveries.

c13.name: Caryll Rune: Formless Oedon
c13.flavor: The Great One Oedon, lacking form, exists only in voice, and is symbolized by this rune.

c14.name: Caryll Rune: Guidance
c14.flavor: Discovered by the old hunter Ludwig. The rune emptied Ludwig of his fears, at least in the midst of a hunt.

c15.name: Hand Lantern
c15.flavor: This hunting accessory provides light while leaving both hands free to hold weapons.

c16.name: Caryll Rune: Hunter
c16.text: Attack gains +1 {speed}. After Attack, may Transform Weapon for free.
c16.flavor: This red-smudged rune means "Hunter", and has been adopted by those who have taken the Hunter of Hunters oath.

c17.name: Hunter Torch
c17.timing: When a non-Boss Enemy moves into your space
c17.text: That Enemy stops 1 space away and suffers 1 {dmg}.
c17.flavor: Designed to incinerate beasts and victims touched by the scourge.

c18.name: Iosefka's Blood Vial
c18.flavor: The product of a careful refinement process, this blood vial appears to be a clinic original.

c19.name: Messenger's Gift
c19.text: Non-Boss Enemies do not Activate after your turn.
c19.flavor: Used to envelop oneself in a black mist, then transform temporarily into a messenger.

c20.name: Caryll Rune: Moon
c20.text: Keep 1 Blood Echo (It is immediately used when you go to the Dream).
c20.flavor: A transcription of "Moon", as spoken by the Great Ones inhabiting the nightmare.

c21.name: Caryll Rune: Oedon Writhe
c21.text: Attack gains Stagger, and may Stagger Attacks of the same Speed.
c21.flavor: "Writhe" acknowledges visceral attacks as one of the darker hunter techniques.

c22.name: Old Hunter Bone
c22.flavor: Allows the art of Quickening, a technique particular to the first Hunters.

c23.name: Tiny Music Box
c23.flavor: A small music box received from a young Yharnam girl. Plays a song shared by her mother and father. Inside the lid is a small scrap of paper, perhaps an old message. Two names can be made out, however faintly, Viola and Gascoigne.

c24.name: Tiny Tonitrus
c24.text: Deal 2 {dmg} to 1 non-Boss Enemy within 1 space. It does not Activate.
c24.flavor: When struck into the ground, it recreates the blue sparks that are said to surround the darkbeasts.

c25.name: A Call Beyond
c25.text: Deal 2 {dmg} to all Enemies in your space.
c25.flavor: Creates a small exploding star, a powerful part of the Choir's arsenal.

c26.name: Beast Roar
c26.text: Move all Enemies in your space up to 2 spaces away.
c26.flavor: Borrow the strength of the terrible undead darkbeasts to blast surrounding foes back.

c27.name: Caryll Rune: Beast
c27.text: Deal 2 {dmg} to 1 other Enemy within 1 space.
c27.flavor: "Beast" is one of the early Caryll Runes, as well as one of the first to be deemed forbidden.

c28.name: Blood Stone Shard
c28.text: Place on an Attack Slot. May switch slot on Transform. While on slot, that Attack deals +1 {dmg}.
c28.flavor: At the workshop, these bloodstones are embedded in weapons to fortify them.

c29.name: Empty Phantasm Shell
c29.flavor: Shells with slime harbouring arcane power, which can be rubbed on weapons to imbue them with their strength.

c30.name: Executioner's Gloves
c30.text: Slay 1 Enemy within 2 spaces that has 2 or less remaining HP.
c30.flavor: The gloves of an executioner from a faraway land.

c31.name: Caryll Rune: Guidance
c31.flavor: Discovered by the old hunter Ludwig. The rune emptied Ludwig of his fears, at least in the midst of a hunt.

c32.name: Hand Lantern
c32.flavor: This hunting accessory provides light while leaving both hands free to hold weapons.

c33.name: Caryll Rune: Hunter
c33.text: Attack gains +1 {speed}. After Attack, may Transform Weapon for free.
c33.flavor: This red-smudged rune means "Hunter", and has been adopted by those who have taken the Hunter of Hunters oath.

c34.name: Iosefka's Blood Vial
c34.flavor: The product of a careful refinement process, this blood vial appears to be a clinic original.

c35.name: Old Hunter Bone
c35.flavor: Allows the art of Quickening, a technique particular to the first Hunters.

c36.name: Ritual Materials
c36.flavor: An exquisite eyeball removed quickly after death, or perhaps even before.

c37.name: Ritual Materials
c37.flavor: An exquisite eyeball removed quickly after death, or perhaps even before.

c38.name: Ritual Materials
c38.flavor: An exquisite eyeball removed quickly after death, or perhaps even before.

c39.name: Ritual Materials
c39.flavor: An exquisite eyeball removed quickly after death, or perhaps even before.

c40.name: Ritual Materials
c40.flavor: An exquisite eyeball removed quickly after death, or perhaps even before.

c41.name: Ritual Materials
c41.flavor: An exquisite eyeball removed quickly after death, or perhaps even before.

c42.name: Ritual Materials
c42.flavor: An exquisite eyeball removed quickly after death, or perhaps even before.

c43.name: Ritual Materials
c43.flavor: An exquisite eyeball removed quickly after death, or perhaps even before.

c44.name: Ritual Materials
c44.flavor: An exquisite eyeball removed quickly after death, or perhaps even before.

c45.name: Ritual Materials
c45.flavor: An exquisite eyeball removed quickly after death, or perhaps even before.

c46.name: Blood Stone Chunk
c46.text: Place on an Attack Slot. May switch slot on Transform. While on slot, that Attack gains +1 {speed} and +1 {dmg}.
c46.flavor: At the workshop, these bloodstones are embedded in weapons to fortify them.

c47.name: Caryll Rune: Corruption
c47.timing: Once per Chapter, if you would be slain
c47.flavor: Several runes contain a nuance of "Blood", associated with the oath of the corrupt.

c48.name: Executioner's Gloves
c48.text: Slay 1 Enemy within 2 spaces that has 2 or less remaining HP.
c48.flavor: The gloves of an executioner from a faraway land.

c49.name: Queenly Flesh
c49.timing: At the Start of Your Turn
c49.flavor: This pinkish lump of flesh remains warm, as if cursed.

c50.name: Caryll Rune: Radiance
c50.text: Draw until you have 3 Stat cards in your hand.
c50.flavor: The rune for "Radiance," adopted by the sworn Executioners under Logarius' command.

c51.name: Ring of Betrothal
c51.flavor: The beings known as the Great Ones imbued this ring with some special meaning.

c52.name: Tear Blood Gem
c52.text: Place on an Attack Slot. May switch slot on Transform. When Attacking with that slot: On Attack: Heal 1
c52.flavor: Created from a shining silver doll tear, this blood gem continually restores the life essence of a hunter.

c53.name: A Call Beyond
c53.text: Deal 2 {dmg} to all Enemies in your space.
c53.flavor: Creates a small exploding star, a powerful part of the Choir's arsenal.

c54.flavor: Borrow the strength of the terrible undead darkbeasts to blast surrounding foes back.
